description: Canonical source manifest for the longevity knowledge tree. pdf_url and alt_url may each be a string or a list of strings; download.py walks pdf_url -> url -> alt_url in order.

sources.title: Biomarkers of Aging Consortium Challenge (portal)
sources.license_note: Consortium portal; subject to agingconsortium.org site terms.

sources.title: An Open Competition for Biomarkers of Aging
sources.license_note: PMC OA primary (PMC11565782); bioRxiv preprint is author-choice.

sources.title: ComputAgeBench: Epigenetic Aging Clocks Benchmark
sources.license_note: bioRxiv primary; KDD 2025 proceedings may differ. GitHub code usually MIT/Apache.

sources.title: Unbiased comparison of 14 epigenetic clocks in relation to 174 incident disease outcomes
sources.license_note: Nature Communications (fully OA).

sources.title: Deep aging clocks: AI-powered strategies for biological age estimation
sources.note: No known open-access version; ScienceDirect paywall. Obtain via institutional access.
sources.license_note: Elsevier (Ageing Research Reviews). Not redistributable.

sources.title: EpInflammAge: Epigenetic-Inflammatory Clock for Disease-Associated Biological Aging Based on Deep Learning
sources.license_note: MDPI IJMS (fully OA).

sources.title: Decoding disease-specific ageing mechanisms through pathway-level epigenetic clock (PathwayAge)
sources.license_note: Lancet eBioMedicine (fully OA).

sources.title: NIA Interventions Testing Program (ITP) — overview
sources.license_note: US Government (NIA/NIH) web page.

sources.title: ITP Data Portal — Mouse Phenome Database
sources.license_note: Jackson Lab Mouse Phenome Database site terms.

sources.title: Dietary restriction impacts health and lifespan of genetically diverse mice
sources.license_note: PMC author-manuscript (NIH public access); Nature version is paywalled.

sources.title: Sex-specific insights into drug-induced lifespan extension and weight loss in mice
sources.license_note: npj Aging (fully OA).

sources.title: Network-driven discovery of repurposable drugs targeting hallmarks of aging
sources.license_note: arXiv default license + PMC version; check arXiv abstract page.

sources.title: Discovery of senolytics using machine learning
sources.license_note: Nature Communications (fully OA). Zenodo code typically MIT-like; check repo.

sources.title: Development and Application of a Senolytic Predictor
sources.license_note: MDPI Molecules (fully OA).

sources.title: Prevalent mesenchymal drift in aging and disease is reversed by partial reprogramming
sources.note: Cell paywall. No preprint known at time of writing.
sources.license_note: Cell (Elsevier). Paywalled; no known preprint at time of writing.

sources.title: Organ-specific proteomic aging clocks predict disease and longevity across diverse populations
sources.note: Nature Aging paywall. No preprint known.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging. Paywalled unless CC-BY paid; verify per-article.

sources.title: A metabolomic profile of biological aging in 250,341 individuals from the UK Biobank
sources.license_note: Nature Communications (fully OA).

sources.title: A comprehensive review of artificial intelligence as a catalyst in aging research
sources.license_note: Frontiers in Aging (fully OA).

sources.title: A single factor for safer cellular rejuvenation (SB000, Shift Bioscience)
sources.license_note: bioRxiv; verify Shift Bioscience's chosen license on the abstract page.

sources.title: Autonomous AI Agents Discover Aging Interventions from Millions of Molecular Profiles (ClockBase Agent)
sources.license_note: PMC OA primary (PMC12667862); bioRxiv preprint author-choice.

sources.title: The cell rejuvenation atlas: leveraging network biology to identify master regulators of rejuvenation strategies (SINGULAR)
sources.license_note: Aging (Albany NY) — CC-BY-3.0 by default.

sources.title: Discovering geroprotectors through the explainable artificial intelligence-based platform AgeXtend
sources.note: Nature Aging published Jan 2025. PMC version may appear later.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging. Paywalled unless CC-BY paid.

sources.title: Determining the age of single cells using scBayesAge
sources.license_note: bioRxiv; verify author license selection.

sources.title: Chemically induced reprogramming to reverse cellular aging (Sinclair lab chemical cocktails)
sources.license_note: Aging (Albany NY) — CC-BY-3.0.

sources.title: Chemical reprogramming ameliorates cellular hallmarks of aging and extends lifespan
sources.license_note: EMBO Mol Med (fully OA) + PMC.

sources.title: In vivo chemical reprogramming is associated with a toxic accumulation of lipid droplets hindering rejuvenation
sources.license_note: bioRxiv primary; Wiley Aging Cell version may be paywalled.

sources.title: Partial cellular reprogramming: A deep dive into an emerging rejuvenation technology
sources.license_note: Wiley Aging Cell. Check OA status per-article; often paywalled.

sources.title: The long and winding road of reprogramming-induced rejuvenation
sources.license_note: Nature Communications (fully OA).

sources.title: Gene Therapy-Mediated Partial Reprogramming Extends Lifespan and Reverses Age-Related Changes in Aged Mice
sources.license_note: PMC author-manuscript (PMC10909732); Liebert Cellular Reprogramming paywalled.

sources.title: In vitro heterochronic parabiosis identifies pigment epithelium-derived factor as a systemic mediator of rejuvenation by young blood
sources.license_note: PMC OA primary (PMC11092633); bioRxiv preprint author-choice.

sources.title: Young CSF restores oligodendrogenesis and memory in aged mice via Fgf17
sources.note: Nature paywall; free-read available via Nature sharable link from paper page.
sources.license_note: Nature (main). Paywalled; no known preprint.

sources.title: Aging insights from heterochronic parabiosis models
sources.license_note: npj Aging (fully OA).

sources.title: A pilot study of senolytics to improve cognition and mobility in older adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease (STAMINA)
sources.license_note: Lancet eBioMedicine (fully OA).

sources.title: Exploring the effects of Dasatinib, Quercetin, and Fisetin on DNA methylation clocks: a longitudinal study on senolytic interventions
sources.license_note: Aging (Albany NY) — CC-BY-3.0.

sources.title: Effects of nicotinamide riboside on NAD+ levels, cognition, and symptom recovery in long-COVID: a randomized controlled trial
sources.license_note: Lancet eClinicalMedicine (fully OA) + PMC.

sources.title: Influence of rapamycin on safety and healthspan metrics after one year: PEARL trial results
sources.license_note: Frontiers in Aging + PMC; medRxiv preprint author-choice.

sources.title: Effect of the mitophagy inducer urolithin A on age-related immune decline: a randomized, placebo-controlled trial
sources.note: Nature Aging paywall.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging. Verify CC-BY OA status per-article.

sources.title: Telomerase reverse transcriptase gene knock-in unleashes enhanced longevity and accelerated damage repair in mice
sources.note: Aging Cell 2025; check Wiley OA status — Aging Cell is increasingly OA.
sources.license_note: Wiley Aging Cell. Verify CC-BY OA status per-article; some articles are OA.

sources.title: Small extracellular vesicles from young adipose-derived stem cells prevent frailty, improve health span, and decrease epigenetic age in old mice
sources.note: Science Advances is fully open access.
sources.license_note: Science Advances (fully OA).

sources.title: XPRIZE Healthspan — $101M global competition
sources.license_note: XPRIZE Foundation website; subject to xprize.org site terms.

sources.title: Inhibition of IL-11 signalling extends mammalian healthspan and lifespan
sources.license_note: Nature (CC-BY by author choice); PMC mirror PMC11291288.

sources.title: Long-term effects of s-KL treatment in wild-type mice: enhancing longevity, physical well-being, and neurological resilience
sources.note: Molecular Therapy 2025 (DOI 10.1016/j.ymthe.2025.02.030). AAV9-s-KL, +20% median lifespan in wild-type mice.
sources.license_note: Molecular Therapy article CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0 (PMC author manuscript OA); classified here as restricted (NC).

sources.title: Precision reprogramming: restoring function to aged cells (Retro Biosciences)
sources.note: Cellular Reprogramming 2025 (DOI 10.1089/cell.2025.0018). AAV-OSK targeted to Cdkn2a+ cells; first Retro publication.
sources.license_note: Liebert Cellular Reprogramming. Paywalled; verify per-article OA status.

sources.title: The End of Aging Clocks: training foundation models to reason in aging and longevity (Longevity-LLM)
sources.license_note: bioRxiv preprint (Insilico Medicine); verify license on abstract page.

sources.title: LongevityBench: are SotA LLMs ready for aging research?
sources.license_note: bioRxiv preprint (Insilico Medicine, Abu Dhabi); verify license on abstract page.

sources.title: scAgeClock: a single-cell transcriptome-based human aging clock model using gated multi-head attention neural networks
sources.license_note: npj Aging (fully OA).

sources.title: Plasma proteomics links brain and immune system aging with healthspan and longevity
sources.note: Nature Medicine 2025; brain & immune-system proteomic age strongest healthspan predictors. bioRxiv preprint open.
sources.license_note: Nature Medicine (paywalled unless CC-BY paid); bioRxiv preprint author-choice.

sources.title: Human clinical trial of plasmapheresis effects on biomarkers of aging (efficacy and safety trial)
sources.note: Negative result: 8 plasmapheresis sessions over 18 weeks did NOT reduce epigenetic age; some clocks accelerated.
sources.license_note: Scientific Reports article notes CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0; classified here as restricted (NC). PMC OA primary.

sources.title: Prophylactic and long-lasting efficacy of senolytic CAR T cells against age-related metabolic dysfunction
sources.license_note: Nature Aging (CC-BY-4.0 by author choice); PMC mirror PMC10950785.

sources.title: Comparative analysis of mouse strains for in vivo induction of reprogramming factors
sources.license_note: Cell Reports (fully OA); bioRxiv preprint.

sources.title: Organ-specific dedifferentiation and epigenetic remodeling in in vivo reprogramming
sources.license_note: Aging Cell (CC-BY-4.0 by author choice); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: Anti-uPAR CAR T cells reverse and prevent aging-associated defects in intestinal regeneration and fitness
sources.note: Amor lab follow-up to senolytic-cart-upar-2024; first CAR-T senolysis demo in gut aging.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging. Verify CC-BY OA status per-article; PMC mirror PMC12823409.

sources.title: Multi-omics analysis reveals biomarkers that contribute to biological age rejuvenation in response to single-blinded randomized placebo-controlled therapeutic plasma exchange
sources.note: Buck Institute RCT (n=42, 50+yo); biweekly TPE+IVIG -2.61 years biological age vs sham.
sources.license_note: Aging Cell (CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: The geroprotectors trametinib and rapamycin combine additively to extend mouse healthspan and lifespan
sources.license_note: Nature Aging (CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: Spatial transcriptomic clocks reveal cell proximity effects in brain ageing
sources.note: Brunet/Rando/Zou; first spatial aging clock — 4.2M brain cells, identifies pro-aging T cells and pro-rejuv NSC neighborhoods.
sources.license_note: Nature article CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0; classified as restricted (NC). PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: A BCL-xL/BCL-2 PROTAC effectively clears senescent cells in the liver and reduces MASH-driven hepatocellular carcinoma in mice
sources.note: First PROTAC senolytic with tissue tropism; avoids navitoclax thrombocytopenia.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging (paywalled); PMC author manuscript OA (PMC12683667).

sources.title: Liver exerkine reverses aging- and Alzheimer's-related memory loss via vasculature
sources.note: Villeda lab follow-up to GPLD1 2020 work; identifies TNAP as the BBB substrate. Exercise-mimetic axis.
sources.license_note: Cell (Elsevier paywall); PMC author manuscript OA (PMC13070421).

sources.title: Human iPSC-derived mononuclear phagocytes improve cognition and neural health across multiple mouse models of aging and Alzheimer's disease
sources.note: Cedars-Sinai/Svendsen; iPSC-derived young immune cells as systemic rejuvenation therapy.
sources.license_note: Advanced Science (Wiley OA, CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA mirror.

sources.title: Single-cell immune aging clocks reveal inter-individual heterogeneity during infection and vaccination
sources.note: scImmuAging: cell-type-specific scRNA-seq aging clocks (n=1,081 donors); shows clock reversibility post-COVID and post-BCG.
sources.license_note: Nature Aging (CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA mirror.

sources.title: X-Atlas/Orion: genome-wide Perturb-seq datasets via a scalable fix-cryopreserve platform for training dose-dependent biological foundation models
sources.note: Xaira Therapeutics; largest published Perturb-seq atlas (8M cells, all human protein-coding genes).
sources.license_note: bioRxiv preprint; dataset under non-commercial license.

sources.title: Semaglutide slows epigenetic aging in people with HIV-associated lipohypertrophy: evidence from a randomized controlled trial
sources.note: First RCT with epigenetic-age outcomes for a GLP-1 agonist; 32-week, n=84.
sources.license_note: medRxiv preprint; verify license on abstract page.

sources.title: Multi-modal atlas of lifestyle interventions reveals malleability of ageing-linked molecular features
sources.note: TirolGESUND lifestyle atlas; n=156, 6mo intermittent fasting or smoking cessation, 4 timepoints, 7 tissues.
sources.license_note: bioRxiv preprint; verify license on abstract page.

sources.title: Association of cardiorespiratory fitness with long-term mortality among adults undergoing exercise treadmill testing
sources.note: Cleveland Clinic retrospective cohort, n=122,007. Low vs elite CRF: HR 5.04 for all-cause mortality. No upper limit of benefit observed.
sources.license_note: JAMA Network Open (fully OA, CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: Aerobic high-intensity intervals improve VO2max more than moderate training
sources.note: Helgerud et al. 2007, Med Sci Sports Exerc. The 4x4 (4 min @ 90-95% HRmax x 4) protocol vs moderate training: +7.2% VO2max in 8 weeks in moderately trained subjects (55.5 to 60.4 mL/kg/min); 15/15 intervals +5.5%; lactate-threshold and LSD groups no significant change.
sources.license_note: Lippincott / ACSM. Paywalled; PubMed abstract free.

sources.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
sources.note: PURE cohort, n=139,691, 17 countries. HR 1.16 per 5 kg lower grip strength for all-cause mortality. Stronger predictor than systolic BP.
sources.license_note: Lancet article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: The association of resistance training with mortality: a systematic review and meta-analysis
sources.note: Resistance training inversely associated with all-cause mortality; additive effect when combined with aerobic exercise.
sources.license_note: Eur J Prev Cardiol article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: Muscular strength as a predictor of all-cause mortality in an apparently healthy population: a systematic review and meta-analysis of data from approximately 2 million men and women
sources.note: Meta-analysis pooled ~2M adults. Higher upper- and lower-body muscular strength associated with lower all-cause mortality, regardless of age or follow-up.
sources.license_note: Arch Phys Med Rehabil article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: Sleep duration and all-cause mortality: a systematic review and meta-analysis of prospective studies
sources.note: Meta-analysis ~1.4M adults across 16 prospective studies. U-shaped: short (<6 hr) RR 1.12; long (>9 hr) RR 1.30 for all-cause mortality.
sources.license_note: Sleep (Oxford) author manuscript via PMC OA.

sources.title: General and abdominal adiposity and risk of death in Europe
sources.note: EPIC cohort, n=359,387, 9.7-yr follow-up. Waist circumference and waist-to-hip ratio independently predict mortality beyond BMI.
sources.license_note: NEJM article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: 21st-century hazards of smoking and benefits of cessation in the United States
sources.note: n=200k US adults. Current smokers lose >10 years of life vs never-smokers; quitting at 25-34 recovers ~10 yrs, at 35-44 recovers ~9 yrs, at 45-54 recovers ~6 yrs.
sources.license_note: NEJM article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: Risk thresholds for alcohol consumption: combined analysis of individual-participant data for 599 912 current drinkers in 83 prospective studies
sources.note: Pooled IPD analysis n=599,912 drinkers. Lowest all-cause mortality threshold ~100 g/week (~7 standard drinks). Cardiovascular subtypes show no safe lower threshold.
sources.license_note: Lancet (CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: A randomized trial of intensive versus standard blood-pressure control
sources.note: SPRINT, n=9,361, non-diabetic hypertensives. SBP target <120 vs <140: 25% reduction in major CV events, 27% reduction in all-cause mortality. Trial halted early for benefit.
sources.license_note: NEJM article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: The effects of lowering LDL cholesterol with statin therapy in people at low risk of vascular disease: meta-analysis of individual data from 27 randomised trials
sources.note: Cholesterol Treatment Trialists (CTT) IPD meta-analysis of 27 RCTs. Each 1 mmol/L LDL-C reduction cuts major vascular events ~21%, including in low-risk primary-prevention subgroups.
sources.license_note: Lancet (CC-BY-4.0); PMC OA primary.

sources.title: A meta-analysis of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, non-high-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and apolipoprotein B as markers of cardiovascular risk
sources.note: Sniderman et al. 2011 Circ Cardiovasc Qual Outcomes. Head-to-head: apoB beats non-HDL-C by 5.7%, beats LDL-C by 12% on relative risk reduction across studies.
sources.license_note: Circ CV Qual Outcomes article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: Vitamin D supplements and prevention of cancer and cardiovascular disease (VITAL)
sources.note: VITAL trial, n=25,871, 5.3 yr follow-up. Vitamin D 2000 IU/day did NOT reduce invasive cancer (HR 0.96), CVD events (HR 0.97), or all-cause mortality (HR 0.99). Disconfirms supplementation in non-deficient adults.
sources.license_note: NEJM article paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: Effect of creatine supplementation during resistance training on lean tissue mass and muscular strength in older adults: a meta-analysis
sources.note: Chilibeck et al. 2017 Open Access J Sports Med. Meta-analysis of 22 RCTs in older adults (n=721, mean age 57+) of creatine + resistance training vs RT alone. Creatine added +1.37 kg lean tissue mass (95% CI 0.97-1.76, p<0.00001) and significantly larger gains in chest press and leg press 1RM. Effect size on the same biomarkers (lean mass, strength) the wiki uses to justify resistance training.
sources.license_note: Open Access J Sports Med (Dove Press) CC-BY-NC.

sources.title: Association between sauna bathing and fatal cardiovascular and all-cause mortality events
sources.note: Laukkanen et al. 2015 JAMA Internal Medicine. KIHD cohort, n=2,315 middle-aged Finnish men, 20.7 yr median follow-up. 4-7 sauna sessions/week vs 1/week: HR 0.37 sudden cardiac death, HR 0.50 fatal CVD, HR 0.60 all-cause mortality. Each additional sauna minute >19 min/session was associated with lower risk.
sources.license_note: JAMA Intern Med paywalled; PubMed abstract free. No PMC OA available.

sources.title: Semaglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in obesity without diabetes (SELECT)
sources.note: Lincoff et al. 2023 NEJM. SELECT trial, n=17,604 patients age 45+ with prior CVD and BMI >=27 without diabetes, randomised 1:1 to semaglutide 2.4 mg/week or placebo. Mean follow-up 39.8 mo. Primary composite endpoint (CV death + nonfatal MI + nonfatal stroke): 6.5% vs 8.0%, HR 0.80 (95% CI 0.72-0.90, p<0.001). Permanent discontinuation due to AEs 16.6% vs 8.2%.
sources.license_note: NEJM paywalled; abstract + figures free.

sources.title: Association of habitual glucosamine use with risk of cardiovascular disease: prospective study in UK Biobank
sources.note: Ma et al. 2019 BMJ. UK Biobank, n=466,039 without CVD at baseline, ~7 yr median follow-up. Habitual glucosamine use: HR 0.85 (95% CI 0.80-0.90) total CVD events, HR 0.78 (0.70-0.87) CVD death, HR 0.82 (0.76-0.88) CHD. Observational; residual confounding plausible since glucosamine users in UK Biobank are systematically different (more health-conscious, exercise more, etc.).
sources.license_note: BMJ open-access, CC-BY-NC.

sources.title: Menopausal hormone therapy and long-term all-cause and cause-specific mortality: the Women's Health Initiative randomized trials
sources.note: Manson et al. 2017 JAMA. 18-yr cumulative follow-up of the WHI hormone-therapy RCTs, n=27,347 postmenopausal women age 50-79 randomised in 1993-1998. All-cause mortality: 27.1% HRT vs 27.6% placebo, HR 0.99 (95% CI 0.94-1.03). CEE+MPA arm HR 1.02; CEE-alone arm HR 0.94. Neutral on mortality overall. Age-stratified: HRs nominally lower in women initiating at 50-59 vs 70-79, supporting the timing hypothesis but not statistically significant for all-cause mortality.
sources.license_note: JAMA paywalled; PMC author manuscript OA.

sources.title: The effect of coenzyme Q10 on morbidity and mortality in chronic heart failure: results from Q-SYMBIO, a randomized double-blind trial
sources.note: Mortensen et al. 2014 JACC Heart Failure. Q-SYMBIO RCT, n=420 chronic HF (NYHA III-IV), CoQ10 100 mg TID vs placebo, 2-yr follow-up. Primary MACE composite: 15% CoQ10 vs 26% placebo, HR 0.50 (p=0.003). Secondary: all-cause mortality HR 0.51 (p=0.018), CV mortality HR 0.43 (p=0.013). HF-specific result; no comparable RCT in healthy adults.
sources.license_note: JACC paywalled; PubMed abstract free.

sources.title: The effect of cold showering on health and work: a randomized controlled trial
sources.note: Buijze et al. 2016 PLOS ONE. n=3,018 adults 18-65, randomised to 30/60/90-second cold shower or no cold shower x 30 days. Cold pooled vs control: 29% reduction in self-reported sickness absence (IRR 0.71, p=0.003). No significant change in number of illness days, only absence. Soft surrogate endpoint; no mortality data.
sources.license_note: PLOS ONE CC-BY-4.0; full text OA.
